tool.name: session_login
tool.description: Store an authenticated session (cookies, headers, tokens) so all agents can reuse it without re-logging in.
tool.details:
After performing a login via curl, browser, or python script, call this tool with the resulting credentials. This prevents every sub-agent from independently re-authenticating, which wastes API calls and risks rate-limiting or account lockouts.
Cookies, headers, and tokens are each semicolon-separated key=value pairs: cookies = "session=abc123; csrf=xyz" headers = "Authorization=Bearer eyJ..." tokens = "access_token=abc; refresh_token=def"

parameter.name: notes
parameter.description: Optional notes about this session (e.g. "admin user with all privileges")

returns.description: Response containing: - success: Whether the session was stored - session_id: The session label - message: Human-readable status

tool.examples:
# Store an admin session after login
admin_login session=abc123def456; csrf_token=xyz789 Admin user logged in via /api/auth/login
# Store a Bearer token auth
api_auth Authorization=Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJ1c2VyIjoiYWRtaW4ifQ access_token=eyJhbGc...; refresh_token=refresh_xyz

tool.name: session_get
tool.description: Retrieve a stored session to reuse its cookies, headers, and tokens in subsequent requests.
tool.details: Call this BEFORE performing authenticated requests to check if a session is already stored. If found, extract the cookies/headers/tokens and include them in your curl/python/browser calls. If not found, perform a fresh login and call session_login to store it.

parameter.name: session_id
parameter.description: The session label to retrieve (must match what was used in session_login)

returns.description: Response containing: - found: True if session exists - session: Dict with cookies, headers, tokens, and metadata - message: Human-readable status if not found

tool.examples:
# Check for an existing admin session
admin_login

tool.name: session_refresh
tool.description: Update an existing stored session with new credentials after a token refresh or re-login.
tool.details: Use this when a session token has expired and you have obtained new credentials. The new values are merged with any existing ones (new values overwrite old ones per-key). If the session does not exist yet, it is created (equivalent to session_login).

parameter.name: notes
parameter.description: Optional notes about the refresh (e.g. "refreshed after 401 on /api/data")

returns.description: Response containing: - success: Whether the update succeeded - action: "updated" or "created" - message: Human-readable status

tool.examples:
# Refresh a session after getting a new token
api_auth access_token=newtoken123; refresh_token=newrefresh456 Refreshed after 401 response on /api/admin/users
